# Phase 6 ops-alert golden file. Plan 06-01 (Wave 0) seeded 5 section
# headers with stub bodies; Plan 06-02 (Wave 1) replaced them with real
# build_ops_alert(...) output produced by the module on 2026-05-12 from
# the fixed inputs documented in
# tests/test_message_builder.py::GOLDEN_SCENARIOS.
#
# 5 scenarios per D-610 + RESEARCH §Pattern 4 + Pitfall A:
#   1. upstream_status_failed  (matcher gate-trip — runs.status='failed')
#   2. xlsx_oversize           (REPORT-06 D-515 cascade — size_guard_passed=False)
#   3. empty_summary_text      (reporter D-507 skip — summary_text='')
#   4. no_xlsx_in_stats        (matcher skipped → no reporter — xlsx_path='')
#   5. delivery_exception      (Pitfall A — TelegramBadRequest/Forbidden/etc.)
#
# Fixed inputs (W1-locked — drift here breaks the golden-file canary):
#   run_id           = 42
#   started_at_utc   = datetime(2026, 5, 11, 17, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
#                      → "2026-05-11 22:00 +0500" Asia/Almaty (UTC+5)
#   viled_count      = 120
#   goldapple_count  = 540
#   size_limit_mb    = 45
#
# Almaty formatting uses strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M %z") — numeric %z (W1
# fix); uppercase %Z would render a platform-dependent named zone.

=== upstream_status_failed ===
🚨 <b>Weekly run #42</b> — upstream pipeline failed

<i>Run started:</i> 2026-05-11 22:00 +0500
<i>Run status:</i> <code>failed</code>
<i>Gate failure:</i> <code>run_status</code>

<i>Snapshot stats:</i>
  viled: 120 • goldapple: 540
  matches: 0 (0.0%)

<i>Manual recovery:</i> <code>python -m ga_crawler deliver-run --run-id 42</code>

=== xlsx_oversize ===
🚨 <b>Weekly run #42</b> — xlsx too large for Telegram

<i>Run started:</i> 2026-05-11 22:00 +0500
<i>Run status:</i> <code>success</code>
<i>Gate failure:</i> <code>size_guard</code>

<i>Snapshot stats:</i>
  viled: 120 • goldapple: 540
  matches: 30 (25.0%)
  xlsx size: 48.7 MB (limit: 45 MB)

<i>Manual recovery:</i> <code>python -m ga_crawler deliver-run --run-id 42</code>
